intro: Ichnocarpus elegans, also known as the "Pakshir" or "Wild Sarsaparilla", is a climbing plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Ichnocarpus elegans include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
